summary: Whitepaper describing VitaDAO's funding of autophagy activator discovery research at the Korolchuk Lab (Newcastle University) through blockchain-based IP-NFT tokenization. The document covers the scientific background, experimental approach, current progress with ~4000 compound screening, and future plans including clinical development pathway for Niemann-Pick Type C and potential expansion to Alzheimer's/Parkinson's.

content_blocks.title: Executive Summary
content_blocks.content: VitaDAO is funding longevity research through sale of VITA-FAST tokens (ERC-20) representing fractions of an IP-NFT for autophagy activator discovery work by the Viktor Korolchuk lab at Newcastle University. Tokens confer governance rights over IP generated by the research. Funded via Sponsored Research Agreement (SRA).

content_blocks.title: Project Overview - Team and Funding Structure
content_blocks.content: VitaDAO owns IP rights to autophagy activator R&D by Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University. Lab led by Dr. Viktor Korolchuk, expert in autophagy and lysosomal biology. Initial funding via Sponsored Research Agreement (SRA) to support investigations into cellular aging mechanisms and drug discovery/development. Lab focuses on identifying therapeutic targets for age-related diseases to improve human healthspan.

content_blocks.title: Project Background - Scientific Rationale
content_blocks.content: Aging is associated with decline in autophagy pathway capacity. Autophagy activation is considered a promising therapeutic approach for aging and age-related diseases. Autophagy is the cell's waste-management system that sequesters, degrades, and recycles faulty cellular components (dysfunctional organelles, misfolded proteins, defective DNA, ROS). Impaired autophagy is strongly linked to neurodegenerative disorders: Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, Huntington's, ALS. Project focuses on autophagy activators targeting the lysosome.

content_blocks.title: Disease Model Selection - Niemann-Pick Type C
content_blocks.content: Niemann-Pick Type C (NPC) chosen as disease model. Rationale: NPC is a monogenetic orphan disease with well-understood biological causes, enabling shorter path to drug approval compared to complex diseases. Once approved for NPC, therapeutic has potential to become recognized treatment for other lysosomal storage disorders and diseases with lysosomal dysfunction (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's). Alzheimer's and Parkinson's have very high incidence of lysosomal dysfunction.

content_blocks.title: Mechanism of Cell Death from Faulty Autophagy
content_blocks.content: Dr. Korolchuk previously contributed to establishing the mechanism by which faulty autophagy leads to cell death: (1) Dysfunctional mitochondria accumulate due to inadequate recycling from faulty autophagy. (2) Accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and DNA damage induces cellular stress. (3) Stress response pathways activated (PARP & SIRT). (4) Depletion of NAD+ and NADH essential to cellular metabolic pathways. (5) Mitochondria lose ability to generate ATP as they become depolarized. (6) Apoptosis (programmed cell death) begins.

content_blocks.title: Cell-Based Assay System
content_blocks.content: Lysosomal dysfunction modeled using cells with mutation in lysosomal protein Niemann-Pick C1 (Npc1-/-) associated with neurodegenerative diseases. When subjected to metabolic stress, these cells suffer cell death due to dysfunctional autophagy, providing easy readout (cells dead/cells alive). To identify true autophagy activators, lab uses cells lacking autophagy initiation (Atg5-/-) in parallel with Npc1-/- cells. Atg5-/- cells are not rescuable by autophagy inducers. This differentiates compounds that specifically activate autophagy (successful in Npc1-/-) from compounds inducing cell survival unrelated to autophagy (successful in Atg5-/-).

content_blocks.title: Experimental Plan - 7-Step Screening and Validation
content_blocks.content: Approximately 4000 bioactive molecules and commercial small molecules to be screened. Naturally occurring molecules consist of unique library of plant and animal compounds from KVP lab. Bioavailability of bioactive compounds useful for drug translation. Plan: (1) Assay optimization and screening in cell survival assays (Atg5-/- vs Npc1-/-) and dose-response, using mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs). (2) Luciferase-p62 clearance assay. (3) Halo orthogonal assay. (4) Selection and purchase of derivatives of lead series. (5) Screening of commercial derivatives in cell survival, p62 clearance, and Halo assay. (6) Synthesis of lead series at CRO and mechanism of action determination. (7) Structure-activity relationship (SAR) and validation in vitro autophagy assays.

content_blocks.title: Cell Survival Assay Details
content_blocks.content: Bioactive molecules and commercial small molecules screened in cell survival assay to identify molecules that can reactivate autophagy in NPC1-/- cells. Assay ensures all molecules advanced are not cytotoxic and therefore suitable for therapeutic development.

content_blocks.title: Luciferase-p62 Clearance Assay
content_blocks.content: Generation and optimization of Npc1-/- MEFs with inducible tet-on-tet-off expression of luciferase-tagged autophagy flux reporter p62. Clearance of p62 protein only possible with proper autophagy. Quantifying clearance confirms autophagy is occurring and at what level.

content_blocks.title: Halo-GFP-LC3 Orthogonal Assay
content_blocks.content: Generation and optimization of Npc1-/- cells stably expressing 'traffic-light' EGFP-RFP-LC3 for high throughput high content screening. Automated image analysis provides information about number of autophagosomes, number of autolysosomes, and whether autophagy has been activated.

content_blocks.title: Current Project Status
content_blocks.content: To date, Dr. Korolchuk and team have generated data on thousands of compounds from initial library using the described assays. From initial screen, several compounds emerged as having potential autophagic properties. Lead series identified, library of derivatives classified and synthesized by external suppliers. Small molecules screened have high chemical variability and no similarity to pre-existing autophagy inducers, representing high possibility of novel IP. At time of publishing, lab is screening second generation of compounds developed as lead series based on hits and waiting on delivery of additional chemical series. These derivatives will undergo same screening process (cell survival, p62 clearance, autolysosome quantification).

content_blocks.title: Next Steps - Drug Translation
content_blocks.content: Once lead molecules identified, VitaDAO and Molecule will work with Newcastle University and Korolchuk lab to start drug translation process. Considerations include: pharmacokinetics, target deconvolution, structure-activity relationships, scalability, and manufacturing. Further research required in in vitro and in vivo models. Possibilities include: Niemann-Pick Type C patient-derived fibroblasts or CRISPR-Cas9 Npc1 knock-out cells to examine efficacy in human cell model. Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) can model disease progression in different cell types. Npc1-mutant mice and cats display same symptoms as humans with Niemann-Pick Type C, providing strong in vivo models for neurological outcomes and potential toxicity evaluation in large animal models.

content_blocks.title: Clinical Development Pathway
content_blocks.content: Phase 1 Clinical Trials: Testing in small group of healthy volunteers or patients (20-100) to evaluate safety, dosage, side effects. Phase 2 Clinical Trials: For Niemann-Pick Type C, Orphan Drug designation could be sought at this stage, providing benefits including tax credits for research and waiver of FDA user fee. Phase 3 Clinical Trials: If successful in Niemann-Pick Type C, drug could be tried for Alzheimer's disease as future indication, though would involve full set of clinical trials. FDA Review and Market: Once Phase 3 complete, New Drug Application (NDA) submitted to FDA. FDA reviews application, can take up to 2 years but may be faster for drugs with Breakthrough Therapy designation.

content_blocks.title: Introduction to IP-NFTs and Blockchain
content_blocks.content: Blockchain is decentralized digital ledger technology for secure and transparent record-keeping. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) use blockchain to verify and authenticate ownership, uniqueness, and provenance. IP-NFTs (Intellectual Property Non-Fungible Tokens) represent intellectual property and data rights to scientific research. IP-NFTs attach legal contracts (e.g., sponsored research agreements) to smart contracts (NFTs) creating new paradigm for scientific research legal contracts. As smart contracts on Ethereum, IP-NFTs can be transferred peer-to-peer, made composable with DeFi, used to distribute governance, built upon to unlock new ways to interact with IP/R&D data/NIPIA (Non-IP Intangible Assets like trade secrets), empower crowd control of commercialization ethics, create unprecedented liquidity in IP markets. First IP-NFTs minted by Molecule for VitaDAO community to register longevity therapeutics IP and R&D data rights on Ethereum.

content_blocks.title: VITA-FAST Token Rights and Structure
content_blocks.content: Molecules represent membership in IP pool containing IP and R&D data attached to parent IP-NFT. Molecules of Korolchuk IP-NFT denoted by token symbol 'VITA-FAST'. Rights governed by Korolchuk-Free Association of Molecules (FAM) Membership Agreement: (1) Governance: right to participate in governance of IP-NFT and development, including voting on proposed licenses and use of proceeds. (2) Access to Intellectual Property: access to relevant data and IP/future IP arising from IP-NFT development for due diligence and informed governance (does not grant license to IP). (3) Duty of Care: honor, support, and adhere to FAM Membership Agreement terms. (4) Confidentiality: must not use or disclose confidential information or IP without consent, or attempt to sell/register Confidential Information, IP, or R&D data rights.

content_blocks.title: Token Distribution
content_blocks.content: Total 1,000,000 VITA-FAST tokens minted. Distribution: 10% (100,000 VITA-FAST) sold to maximum 499 VITA tokenholders, vesting over 2 months with 2 month cliff. VitaDAO retains 69%. Researchers receive 5% vested over 4 years with 1 year cliff (incentivizes long-term success, discourages data falsification). Newcastle University receives 1% vested over 4 years with 1 year cliff (not required in contract but intended as signal to other University TTOs and incentive to speed licensing process). VitaDAO dealflow working group contributors receive 5% vested over 4 years with 1 year cliff (incentivizes long-term success, must be approved by VitaDAO governance). Liquidity pool receives 10%. Uniswap V3 liquidity pool created upon claiming sale proceeds, matching 10% of VITA-FAST tokens with 10% of funds raised.

content_blocks.title: Token Distribution Pie Chart
content_blocks.content: Visual representation of token allocation: VitaDAO (liquid) 69%, Genesis Sale (liquid) 10%, Liquidity Pool (liquid) 10%, Newcastle University (vested) 1%, Researchers (vested) 5%, VitaDAO DFWG Members (vested) 5%.

content_blocks.title: Genesis Sale Structure
content_blocks.content: Fixed-price sale with pro rata distribution, overflow refunds, and 2 month vesting. 10% of VITA-FAST tokens sold to VITA holders at fixed price determined by cost-based valuation. Funds raised in wrapped ether (WETH) to minimize USD(C) inflation impact and minimize swaps required by participants.

content_blocks.title: Cost-Based Valuation
content_blocks.content: Price per VITA-FAST token determined by cost-based valuation that does not factor in any appreciation/depreciation based on emergent data. VITA-FAST token buyers/sellers have power of price discovery in open market once liquidity seeded. Valuation calculated by adding: VitaDAO's committed research costs 220,358 CHF (245,557 USD), incurred legal and operational costs 38,563 CHF (43,295 USD), inflation 23,865 USD (calculated with US Bureau of Statistics Inflation Calculator). Total cost-based price: 312,717 USD for all 1,000,000 VITA-FAST tokens. Therefore 10% of Molecules valued at 31,271 USD and price of 0.31271 USD per VITA-FAST token.

content_blocks.title: Bidding Mechanism and VITA Staking
content_blocks.content: Sale occurs over 2-day period where VitaDAO members may bid as much as willing to contribute. To bid, bidder must lock (stake) VITA equivalent to amount of ETH they want to bid. Buyer's bid value can be up to maximum of their VITA holdings locked in sales contract. More VITA staked for 60-day vesting period enables larger bid during 2-day sale. If sales goal of 31,271 USD not met, all funds returned to funders.

content_blocks.title: Pro Rata Distribution and Overflow Refunds
content_blocks.content: If sales goal met or exceeded, final allocation of Molecules proportional to each bidder's fraction of total bids. Example: If Alice bids 40,000 USD and Bob bids 20,000 USD (only funders), Alice receives twice the allocation as Bob (also means Alice staked twice as much VITA as Bob). Any overflow returned to bidders. If amount bid exceeds sales goal, excess automatically returned via token sale smart contract. Example: If VITA-FAST sought to raise 31,271 USD but Alice and Bob together bid 60,000 USD, surplus of 28,279 USD overflows and returned pro rata (Alice gets back 19,153 USD, Bob gets back 9,576 USD).

content_blocks.title: Vesting Period
content_blocks.content: Sale participants receive vested VITA (vVITA) and vested VITA-FAST (vVITA-FAST) to ensure they can participate in governance while vesting. After two-month (60 day) vesting period, participants can swap vVITA for VITA and vVITA-FAST for VITA-FAST tokens. Tokens locked for full 2-month vesting period.

content_blocks.title: VITA-FAST Molecules Crowdsale Flow Diagram
content_blocks.content: Illustration of fixed price token sale with pro rata distribution and overflow refunds. Shows 2-day sale period (Day 0) with funding goal 16 ETH, VITA-FAST Price 0.0016 ETH, VITA Price 0.001 ETH, VITA-FAST for Sale 1,000. Bob example: Bids 5 ETH + stakes 5,000 VITA. Alice example: Bids 15 ETH + stakes 15,000 VITA. At vesting (60 days), Bob claims 250 VITA-FAST + 4,000 vVITA with refund 1 ETH + 1,000 VITA. Alice claims 750 vVITA-FAST + 12,000 vVITA with refund 3 ETH + 3,000 VITA. After unwrap period, Bob unlocks 250 VITA-FAST + 4,000 VITA, Alice unlocks 750 VITA-FAST + 12,000 VITA.

content_blocks.title: Use of Funds - Initial and Governed
content_blocks.content: Immediate application of raised funds: establish liquidity for VITA-FAST. VITA-FAST holders have power to decide how funds subsequently used in advancing longevity research and IP development at Korolchuk lab. Example use case: Decentralization of Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) analysis. Korolchuk lab has computational biologist on staff, but objective is to harness superior SAR expertise from global pool. Aim: identify promising compound scaffolds and design optimal candidates for medicinal chemistry optimization. LabDAO proposed providing access to extensive network of computational chemists and creating hackathons centered around analyzing Korolchuk lab data. Given vast array of compounds identified as hits, both SAR analyses and medicinal chemistry indispensable in lead compound selection.

content_blocks.title: Additional Use of Funds Examples
content_blocks.content: Other possible fund applications: Engaging experienced medicinal chemists. Allocation of funds toward filing patents. Given potential volume of novel compounds generated, robust substance of matter patent protection for most promising compounds vital to enhance IP value. While short-term use of raised capital for ensuring liquidity, through token holder governance various pathways to redirect capital from liquidity to IP generation. This transition should be community decision where VITA-FAST holders have necessary data and context to facilitate informed decision-making.

content_blocks.title: Risk Factors
content_blocks.content: Liquidity Risk: Market for trading Molecules might be limited, may not resell easily or at satisfactory price. Regulatory Risk: Regulatory landscape for Molecules and blockchain evolving, changes in laws/regulations could materially impact value or legality. Project Risk: Success of scientific research not guaranteed, delays, cost overruns, or research might not yield expected results. Technological Risk: Risk of loss due to hacking, technical glitches, or blockchain issues. Smart Contract Risk: Contract could have bugs or security vulnerabilities. Market Risk: Value could fluctuate due to changes in broader market for NFTs, biotechnology IP, or cryptocurrencies. Intellectual Property Risk: May be disputes or uncertainties regarding ownership or enforcement of underlying IP.

content_blocks.title: Additional Risk Factors
content_blocks.content: Legal and Compliance Risk: Depending on jurisdiction, owning and trading Molecules might have legal implications including potential tax liabilities. Network Risk: Value and function could be impacted by changes or issues with Ethereum blockchain (changes in consensus mechanism, forks, network congestion). Operational Risk: Platforms or exchanges used to buy, sell, or store Molecules could have operational issues (downtime) impacting ability to manage purchase. Governance Risk: Molecules include governance rights, may be disagreements or disputes among token holders.

content_blocks.title: Legal Considerations - Token Holder Rights
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST represents membership in IP pool containing Discovering Novel Autophagy Activators IP-NFT and attached IP and R&D data. IP pool membership enables token holders to govern joint development and experimental use of IP and R&D data in pool. VITA-FAST holders may have rights to govern issuance of licenses to use underlying IP and R&D data attached to IP-NFT. Control over licensing function decentralized through token holder governance. VITA-FAST holders may have rights to govern proceeds from licensing or sale of IP and R&D data. Distribution of licensing or sale proceeds not hard-coded. Given regulatory uncertainty, token holders must make careful judgment in future about whether and how to distribute proceeds (such as by forming legal entity prior to distribution, recommended to solicit legal advice).

content_blocks.title: Information Rights and Data Access
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST holders may be granted information rights: regular non-confidential updates from researchers and ability to pose queries. Access to data rooms containing IP and R&D data, including continuously open non-confidential data room. Designed to aid community decision-making through provision of research results, with proprietary data redacted to safeguard IP integrity. Data room serves as primary means for delivering non-confidential updates to Molecule holders. VITA-FAST holders may have opportunity to gain access to confidential data, subject to adherence to protocols such as KYC and NDAs as governed by token holders. Confidential data highly proprietary, requiring protocols to safeguard IP.

content_blocks.title: U.S. Securities Compliance
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST will not be sold to U.S. persons in compliance with Regulation S of United States Securities Act of 1933. VITA-FAST will not be made available to U.S. persons or within United States. Potential purchasers required to provide sufficient proof of non-U.S. person status including IP address checks, geofencing, and attestations.

content_blocks.title: Team - Viktor Korolchuk
content_blocks.content: Viktor Korolchuk is cell biologist and researcher leading Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University. PhD from University of Cambridge in 2007. Postdoctoral positions at University of California San Diego and University of Cambridge. Research focuses on molecular mechanisms regulating cellular homeostasis, particularly in context of neurodegenerative diseases and cancer. Published numerous articles in high-impact scientific journals, recognized as expert in field. Involved in several industry collaborations, received funding from UK Medical Research Council, European Research Council, and Wellcome Trust. Member of Biochemical Society and Royal Society of Biology.

content_blocks.title: Team - Peter Banks
content_blocks.content: Peter Banks trained as geneticist during undergraduate degree at Newcastle University. PhD studying role of cohesins in Caenorhabditis elegans at Newcastle University. Postdoctoral role with David Lydall studying chromatin modifications in budding yeast, became interested in use of robotics in genetic research. Developed skills in high throughput screening using unique setup developed by David Lydall within Centre for Integrated Systems Biology of Ageing and Nutrition. In 2011, appointed to run new facility in Faculty of Medical Sciences at Newcastle University. Initially offered genome wide yeast screens, faculty invested significant funds to develop mammalian siRNA screening in 2012. Screening facility continued to develop, recruiting Adrian Blackburn and incorporating bacteria and alternative yeasts such as Candida albicans.

content_blocks.title: Team - Jóhannes Reynisson
content_blocks.content: Jóhannes Reynisson is drug discovery expert and research lead at VitaDAO, oversees projects related to drug discovery and leads collaborations with academic institutions and industry partners. PhD in chemical biology from University of Copenhagen. Extensive experience in drug discovery projects, including working as molecular modeler at Institute of Cancer Research London UK and project leader at University of Auckland NZ. Extensive pharmaceutical industry experience including medicinal chemist and project leader at Novartis and AstraZeneca. Authored more than 150 peer-reviewed publications and patents. Research focuses on discovery of new drug candidates and development of novel therapeutic strategies for various diseases including cancer, infectious diseases, and neurodegenerative disorders.

content_blocks.title: Additional Support and Resources
content_blocks.content: In addition to core team, project has support from entire VitaDAO community of contributors. Resources listed: Korolchuk Lab - VitaDAO, Discovering Novel Autophagy Activators - Molecule, Funding Science Through Blockchain Technology and Cryptocurrencies - Newcastle University, Newcastle fractionalization proposal to VitaDAO (VDP-100), VitaDAO snapshot proposal approving VDP-100, Korolchuk-Free Association of Molecules (FAM) Membership Agreement.

key_findings: Project at early-stage drug discovery (TRL 2-3): screening ~4000 bioactive and commercial small molecules for autophagy activation using cell-based assays
Multiple validated assays established: cell survival assay (Npc1-/- vs Atg5-/- MEFs), luciferase-p62 clearance assay, Halo-GFP-LC3 traffic-light assay for autophagosome/autolysosome quantification
Initial screening complete: thousands of compounds tested, several lead series identified with potential autophagic properties, second generation derivatives currently under evaluation
Novel IP potential: screened molecules have high chemical variability and no similarity to pre-existing autophagy inducers
Disease model: Niemann-Pick Type C (monogenetic lysosomal storage disorder) chosen as development path with potential expansion to Alzheimer's/Parkinson's
Clinical pathway defined: Orphan Drug designation strategy for Phase 2, potential Breakthrough Therapy designation to accelerate FDA review

Mechanism understood: Dr. Korolchuk previously contributed to establishing mechanism by which faulty autophagy leads to cell death via mitochondrial dysfunction, ROS accumulation, NAD+ depletion, and apoptosis
Next steps: pharmacokinetics, target deconvolution, SAR, lead optimization, then patient-derived cells, iPSCs, and animal models (Npc1-mutant mice and cats)

Team expertise: Dr. Viktor Korolchuk (autophagy/lysosomal biology expert, Cambridge PhD), Peter Banks (high-throughput screening specialist), Jóhannes Reynisson (drug discovery expert, 150+ publications)
Funding structure: VitaDAO owns IP rights via Sponsored Research Agreement, ~$312k cost-based valuation (220,358 CHF research costs + 38,563 CHF legal/operational costs + inflation)
IP structure: IP-NFT on Ethereum with fractional governance tokens (VITA-FAST), token holders vote on licensing, use of proceeds, and development decisions
No U.S. sales: Regulation S compliance, geofencing and attestations required
